audit_items.proof_trace: CloneKernelInfo() can return NULL on memory allocation failure (AcquireMagickMemory at L2223 or AcquireAlignedMemory at L2231). In ExpandRotateKernelInfo (L2424-2426), the return value is used immediately as RotateKernelInfo(clone, angle) and SameKernelInfo(kernel, clone) with no NULL guard; both callees dereference the pointer at their first line (L4231 and L2393 respectively). Other callers of CloneKernelInfo in the same file (L3769, L4154) properly check for NULL, confirming this omission is an oversight.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker provides a crafted image processed via a morphology operation with a kernel rotation flag (> or @); under memory pressure the CloneKernelInfo call inside ExpandRotateKernelInfo returns NULL, which is immediately dereferenced by RotateKernelInfo or SameKernelInfo, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash (denial of service).
